mnemonics.title: DP Table Size
mnemonics.content: NPW: N items times Packed Weight gives table size. Remember: 'N'umber of 'P'roblems 'W'orth solving = N×W cells.

mnemonics.title: Recurrence Logic
mnemonics.content: MAXimum VALUE: Either skip it (leave money on table) or take it (value + profit from remaining space). MAX of both gives profit.

mnemonics.title: Backward Iteration
mnemonics.content: Go BACK to prevent BACK counting: Iterate backward so each item is used only once.

mnemonics.title: Greedy Fails
mnemonics.content: Greedy GGrab grabs value/weight but doesn't guarantee Gottttt (Got the total best). 0/1 needs DP not Greedy.

acronyms.usage: Solving Knapsack by breaking into overlapping subproblems
Maximum weight the knapsack can carry

acronyms.expansion: Zero or One (take or not take)
acronyms.usage: Each item can be selected at most once

visualTips.visualization: Think of a grid: rows = items (0 to n), columns = capacity (0 to W). Fill each cell with maximum profit for that combination.
At each cell, imagine a fork: one path goes straight (don't take item), another goes up-left then adds value. Take the bigger one.
Instead of table, use a single row. When processing an item, fill from right to left - like painting over old values without disturbing already computed results for current item.
Start from bottom-right corner, walk backwards: if value changes, item was taken (move up-left), if stays same, item wasn't taken (move up).

analogies.analogy: Imagine a thief with one bag that can hold limited weight. Each jewelry item has weight and value - either take the whole item or leave it, like a once-in-a-lifetime decision.
Like dividing a gold bar - you can take any fraction of an item. It's like shopping with a scale where you can buy portions.
DP is like a student solving a puzzle by remembering answers to sub-questions, while pure recursion is like re-solving the same sub-question every time from scratch.
Instead of keeping all historical snapshots of your work, you only keep the most recent relevant calculation, like summarizing a story in one sentence.
